What is this simulator?
This tool answers: "What if this token had been launched through Floors Protocol instead of a regular DEX?"
● Actual price — the real historical price of the token as it traded on DEXes (Aerodrome, Uniswap). This is what actually happened — the wild pumps, crashes, and everything in between.
● Floors market price — the simulated price if the same trading volume had flowed through a Floors bonding curve instead. Floors absorbs volatility: buys push price up through tiers, sells push it back down, but fees from every trade permanently elevate the floor.
● Floor price — the permanent, guaranteed minimum price. It can only go up, never down. Funded by trading fees, idle-reserve yield, and LRE (Liquidity Reallocation Elevation). This is the "worst case" for any Floors token holder.
Real tokens (VVV, VIRTUAL) use actual on-chain trade data. Synthetic scenarios use generated trade histories modeling different market regimes (healthy growth, boom & bust, slow death). Toggle LRE presets to see how active liquidity management affects floor growth.
